title: Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Male Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Eastern Ghats and is located in the state of Karnataka in India.
overview.description: Male Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Eastern Ghats and is located in the state of Karnataka in India. It is named after the presiding deity "Lord Male Mahadeshwara" of the famed Male Mahadeshwara Hills located within the sanctuary. The sanctuary lies in the Chamarajanagar district of Karnataka. It is at a distance of 140 km (87 mi) from Mysore and 210 km (130 mi) from Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: South Deccan Plateau dry deciduous forests
topPlaces.description: The South Deccan Plateau dry deciduous forests is a tropical dry forest ecoregion in southern India. The ecoregion lies in the southernmost portion of the Deccan Plateau, and includes the southernmost portion of the Eastern Ghats.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (91 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (96 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Malai Mahadeshwara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are South Deccan Plateau dry deciduous forests.
